# CWE-257 positive: reversible AES encryption for passwords (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-257-vulnerable.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"crypto/rand"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/base64"
	"encoding/json"
	"io"
	"net/http"
)

// CWE-257 Storing Passwords in a Recoverable Format
// Vulnerable line: encrypts password with static key for later decryption.
func PersistLoginSecret(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB, key []byte) {
	plain := []byte(r.FormValue("pass"))
	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "crypto", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "crypto", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())
	if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "crypto", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nil, nonce, plain, nil)
	ciphertext = append(nonce, ciphertext...)
	encoded := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(ciphertext)
	login := r.FormValue("login")
	_, err = db.Exec("INSERT INTO logins(login, secret) VALUES(?, ?)", login, encoded)
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "save", http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return
	}
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"status": "stored"})
}
